title: pilosella caespitosa subsp. leptocaulon
intro: Pilosella caespitosa subsp. leptocaulon, also known as Alpine Mouse-ear or Alpine Mouse-ear Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which help in treating various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and digestive problems. The plant is rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function. Pilosella caespitosa subsp. leptocaulon can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are commonly used in traditional herbal medicine.
